title: South Boston woman fatally stabbed boyfriend during an argument, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, pleaded not guilty to manslaughter for allegedly fatally stabbing her boyfriend during an argument in South Boston.
Police identified the victim as 26-year-old Kyle Bradford of Cambridge; Pascual claims self-defense citing his prior threats.
Pascual was held on $100,000 bond; Bradford had a history of dismissed domestic assault charges against him in Massachusetts and Georgia.

title: Patriots to be featured on HBO’s ‘Hard Knocks’ ahead of 2027 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots will be featured on HBO's 'Hard Knocks' series in 2027.
The Seattle Seahawks will appear on 'Hard Knocks' ahead of the 2026 NFL season.
Eligibility rule changes now allow playoff teams like the Patriots to be featured on 'Hard Knocks'.

title: James Hagens notches first points with Providence Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens scored his first pro goal and assisted in back-to-back games for the Providence Bruins.
Hagens debuted on Providence's top line and later moved to a skilled second line with Matthew Poitras and Georgii Merkulov.
Providence coach Ryan Mougenel praised Hagens' skating and playmaking; potential NHL promotion depends on his performance.

title: Could Patriots circle back with Stefon Diggs? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said of a potential reunion.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots released Stefon Diggs due to a $26.8 million cap hit, seeking younger talent like Romeo Doubs.
Executive Eliot Wolf stated potential Diggs reunion isn't ruled out if circumstances change like injuries or shifts.
Diggs led Patriots with 85 catches in 2025 but struggled in the postseason, raising questions about his future role.

title: Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was sentenced to 50 years for raping two women in a Framingham store in 1989.
Gale was convicted on charges including aggravated rape and armed robbery after being identified through DNA in a cold case.
Victims delivered impact statements at the sentencing, and Gale has filed a notice of appeal following the court decision.

title: Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning coach of the year: ‘It’s a stupid award’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla called the NBA Coach of the Year award 'a stupid award' and prefers focus on players and staff.
Mazzulla is a Coach of the Year contender after leading the Celtics to a top Eastern Conference record despite losing Jayson Tatum.
Mazzulla emphasizes personal development and team contribution over individual accolades, focusing on helping and supporting players.

title: 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: One person died in a car crash in Mattapan late Sunday night on American Legion Highway.
The crash occurred at 11:14 p.m. at the intersection of Walk Hill Street and Canterbury Street.
The victim was found in the car, pronounced dead at the scene, with significant car damage reported.

title: Should Joe Mazzulla win Coach of the Year? Here’s how he stacks up against the competition.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla has the second-best odds to win NBA Coach of the Year, behind Detroit's J.B. Bickerstaff.
The Celtics achieved a strong season despite losing several key players and have a top-five record in the NBA.
The Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference with a focus on player development and improved rebound performance.

title: Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A 24-foot 'End ICE' ice sculpture on Boston Common was destroyed hours after the 'No Kings' rally.
Artist Kat Dressler designed the sculpture to convey a message even if vandalized; it was knocked over twice.
Boston police responded to the scene but no report was filed; other nearby protest materials were also damaged.

title: Worcester man gets 30 years in prison for sexually exploiting minors
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Antonio Merced, 29, from Worcester, sentenced to 30 years for exploiting minors, pleaded guilty to charges in 2025.
Merced's federal sentence for sexually abusing three children will run concurrently with prior state sentences of 25-30 years.
Merced admitted possessing hundreds of child pornography images and videos of known and unknown minors, prosecutors reported.
